name: Ontur and Albotana — circular from Tob
name_original: Ontur y Albotana — circular desde Tobarra
description: This moderate 35.3-mile road cycling route from Tobarra offers quiet roads, scenic landscapes, and a consistent challenge with 1430 feet of
region_intro: Road cycling around Tobarra features a landscape characterized by rolling terrain and agricultural areas, with routes often connecting historic towns and natural landmarks. The region includes varied elevations, from gentle climbs to more challenging ascents, providing diverse experiences for road cyclists. Routes frequently pass through open countryside and offer views of the local topography, including notable features like volcanic plugs and river valleys. Best road cycling routes around Tobarra The most popular road cycling route is Ontur and Albotana — circular from Tob, a 35.3 miles (56.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 23 minutes to complete. This moderate route includes a significant elevation gain, traversing varied rural landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Hellín – Cancarix Volcanic Plug loop from Hellin, a moderate 28.5 miles (45.9 km) path. This route offers views of the unique Cancarix Volcanic Plug and surrounding countryside. Local road cyclists also love the Hellín – View of Hellín at Sunset loop from Hellin, a 21.0 miles (33.9 km) trail leading through the area around Hellín, often completed in about 1 hour 26 minutes. This easy route provides accessible road cycling options. Road cycling around Tobarra is defined by its mix of agricultural plains, rolling hills, and routes connecting historic settlements. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy road bike trails near Tobarra to more advanced road cycling trails Tobarra. The routes in Tobarra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Tobarra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Tobarra?
A: There are over 30 road cycling routes around Tobarra, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 6 easy routes, 18 moderate routes, and 8 difficult routes, catering to various skill levels.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes for beginners in Tobarra?
A: Yes, Tobarra offers several easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38173432" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hellín – View of Hellín at Sunset loop from Hellin&lt;/a&gt;, a 21.0-mile (33.9 km) route that provides accessible road cycling options through the area around Hellín.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for advanced riders in Tobarra?
A: For advanced riders seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38224018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Pilar Fountain – Liétor Old Town loop from Hellin&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gains and longer distances. This particular route covers 38.1 miles (61.3 km) with substantial climbing, providing a demanding experience.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Tobarra?
A: Many road cycling routes in Tobarra are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167535894/ontur-and-albotana-circular-from-tob" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ontur and Albotana — circular from Tob&lt;/a&gt;, a 35.3-mile (56.9 km) route that traverses varied rural landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Tobarra?
A: Road cycling around Tobarra features a landscape characterized by rolling terrain and agricultural areas. Routes often connect historic towns and natural landmarks, with varied elevations from gentle climbs to more challenging ascents. You'll frequently pass through open countryside with views of local topography, including volcanic plugs and river valleys.

Q: What are some interesting places or landmarks to see along road cycling routes in Tobarra?
A: Many routes offer views of unique natural features and historic sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40306761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hellín – Cancarix Volcanic Plug loop from Hellin&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, provides views of the distinctive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5625950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cancarix Volcanic Plug&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5647244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hellín&lt;/a&gt; and the town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5819002" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tobarra&lt;/a&gt; itself.

Q: Are there any specific attractions near the road cycling routes in Tobarra?
A: Beyond the routes, you can explore attractions such as the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2867722" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de los Patos&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5589755" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary Hermitage of the Encarnación&lt;/a&gt;, or the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5625950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eremitorio Rupestre de Alborajico&lt;/a&gt;. These highlights offer cultural and natural points of interest to complement your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Tobarra?
A: The region generally offers pleasant conditions for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide mild temperatures, making them ideal for longer rides. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for families in Tobarra?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designated routes aren't highlighted, the 6 easy-grade routes around Tobarra are generally suitable for less experienced riders or families looking for a more relaxed pace. These routes tend to have less elevation gain and shorter distances.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Tobarra?
A: Many routes start from towns like Tobarra or Hellín, where public parking is generally available. It's advisable to check local parking regulations and availability in the specific starting town for your chosen route.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Tobarra?
A: The road cycling routes in Tobarra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the mix of agricultural plains and rolling hills, and the opportunity to connect with historic settlements.

Q: How long do road cycling routes around Tobarra typically take to complete?
A: The duration of road cycling routes around Tobarra varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. Easy routes, like the 21.0-mile (33.9 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38173432" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hellín – View of Hellín at Sunset loop from Hellin&lt;/a&gt;, can be completed in about 1 hour 26 minutes, while longer, more challenging routes can take over 3 hours.

Q: Can I find road cycling routes that pass through historic towns or settlements?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Tobarra are designed to connect historic towns and settlements. You'll find routes that lead through or offer views of places like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5647244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hellín&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5819002" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tobarra&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to experience the local culture and history as you ride.
tips: Hellín is the most populated municipality in the province, after Albacete. Among its heritage, several churches and sanctuaries stand out: Archpriestal Church of Santa María de la Asunción, Conventual Church of Nuestra Señora de los Ángeles - Franciscan Fathers, Sanctuary of Nuestra Señora del Rosario, Convent of the Poor Clares (House of Culture) etc. Stop by the Plaza del Anfiteatro to see the old water mill and have a drink. The celebrations of Holy Week (Tamborada) have since 2007 the title "Fiesta of International Tourist Interest".
Tobarra offers a complete experience: historical heritage, unique traditions, nature, and authentic cuisine. Perfect for both a day trip and a more relaxed stay 🙂

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This road cycling route is approximately 56.9 kilometers long. On average, cyclists can expect to complete it in about 2 hours and 24 minutes, depending on their pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Ontur y Albotana — circular desde Tobarra route?
faqs.answer: The route features mostly well-paved surfaces, making it ideal for road bikes. While classified as moderate, it includes a consistent elevation gain of about 436 meters, suggesting a steady challenge. The broader region is known for some climbs with significant inclines.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: The route is classified as 'moderate' due to its length and elevation gain. While it's well-paved, the consistent climbing might be challenging for absolute beginners. Cyclists should have good fitness to comfortably complete it.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the way, you can enjoy tranquil cycling environments with potentially beautiful landscapes and quiet roads, characteristic of the low-population density around Albatana and Castilla-La Mancha. Notable points include the town of Tobarra and a View of Hellín at Sunset.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting from Tobarra. You can typically find parking within the town of Tobarra to begin your ride. Specific trailhead parking details are not provided, but public parking in the town would be the most convenient option.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Castilla-La Mancha region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions outside of the peak summer heat. Spring and autumn are often ideal, providing milder temperatures and comfortable riding weather. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this is a road cycling route primarily on paved roads, dogs are generally not recommended to accompany cyclists for their safety and the safety of others. If you are considering bringing a dog, ensure they are securely leashed and able to keep pace without risk, though it's not typically a dog-friendly activity.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Ontur y Albotana — circular desde Tobarra route. It uses public roads and is freely accessible.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: While specific cafes are not listed, the route passes through or near towns like Tobarra, Ontur, and Albatana. These towns are likely to offer opportunities for refreshments, water, and food. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer rides.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other paths, including Sendero del Mundo, Camino del Cuco de la Guardia Civil a Sierra, Cañada Real de la Mancha a Murcia, Cerro San Cristóbal, and Camino de las Columnas.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is best suited for this route?
faqs.answer: Given that it's a road cycling route with mostly well-paved surfaces, a road bike is the most suitable choice for this trail. Its design allows for efficient travel over the 56.9 kilometers and 436 meters of elevation gain.
